statement.markdown: Write a function that checks if a given sentence is a pangram, i.e., if it contains all the letters of the alphabet at least once. A sentence is also a pangram if it contains uppercase and lowercase versions of the same letter. The function should return `True` if the sentence is a pangram, and `False` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string containing a sentence.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a boolean value.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input sentence only contains letters, spaces, and punctuation. Also, you can assume that the sentence is not empty.

examples.input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
examples.explanation: This sentence is a pangram because it contains all the letters of the alphabet.
This sentence is not a pangram because it does not contain all the letters of the alphabet.

hints.text: Convert the input sentence to lowercase to simplify the problem.

editorial.title: Correct Approach
editorial.intuition: We can use a set to keep track of the unique letters found in the sentence.
editorial.bruteForce: We can iterate over each character in the sentence and check if it is a letter. If it is, we add it to the set.
editorial.betterSolution: We can convert the sentence to lowercase and iterate over each unique letter in the alphabet.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a set to keep track of the unique letters found in the sentence.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can use the set to check if all the letters of the alphabet are present in the sentence.

testCases.input:
5
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Hello World
Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs
The five boxing wizards jump quickly at dawn
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog

6
Hello World
This is a test
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs
The five boxing wizards jump quickly at dawn
This is another test
testCases.output:
NO
NO
YES
YES
YES
NO
